mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and personal savings deposits indicates robust consumer spending capacity combined with elevated precautionary balances, which fuels corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative signal regimes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial production growth, supply bottlenecks elevate input costs and compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO growth falls below the 25th percentile, or rolling 3-year IC drops below -0.05 only during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect increased business investment and consumer confidence in the real estate sector, which historically correlates with positive equity market momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the point-in-time Rank IC fails to exceed 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to suppress corporate valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 4.5% for consecutive quarters, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-rate and low-rate regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and rising consumer price inflation signals an overheating economy where wage-push inflation erodes real margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over individual series, or forward returns are not significantly lower when PAYEMS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, rapid increases in real GDP combined with stable inflation expectations drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3% annualized.
